name: Oat Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2-3 cups turbinado sugar (I used 3 last night, down from 4, want to reduce more)
2 cups rolled oats (process in food processor to coarse flour)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix well the soy yogurt and applesauce.
2. Sift together 1 cup unbleached flour, 1.5 tablespoons baking powder, 1.5 tablespoons baking soda, 1 teaspoon salt, and 2 teaspoons ener-g egg replacer into the first mixture. Stir until a cookie dough texture is reached (I had to use approximately 2.75 cups total).
3. Add chips or fruit or whatever desired.
4. Shape into balls and bake at 375°F until browned. The cookies do not melt around the edges in an unusual way, indicating they are successful.
Note: While these cookies contain less fat than traditional versions, they may still be considered a "sugar bomb" due to their high sugar content.
